description_long: This is a rustic wooden cabin with a prominent gable roof constructed from weathered timber planks. The front facade features a small porch area with a shingled overhang supported by simple posts, and the wall is clad in a mix of horizontal and shingle-style wood textures. The side walls are made of long, vertically aligned wooden boards, giving the structure a sturdy, utilitarian appearance. The roof is steeply pitched and extends well beyond the walls, providing ample protection from the elements. There are no visible windows, and the only entrance appears to be on the porch side. The overall look is that of a traditional, rural cabin suited for natural or park-like urban settings.
description: A rustic wooden cabin with a steep gable roof, shingled porch, and weathered timber walls, featuring a single entrance and no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the shingled porch, entrance, and detailed wood textures on the facade.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long, vertical wooden planks and the full length of the overhanging gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the plain wooden wall and the symmetrical gable roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with vertical wood siding and a clear view of the porch structure at the far end.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with steep pitch and extended overhangs, constructed from wooden planks
attribute_window_arrangement: No visible windows on any side; the cabin appears windowless from all views provided
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door located on the front facade, opening onto the porch area
